messages.author: FinMachJones
messages.text: A firmware selector for the Heltec WiFi LoRa 32 V4.2 (ESP32-S3) that lets you switch between multiple firmwares at boot time — Meshtastic, MeshCore, Reticulum/RNode, or anything else. https://github.com/Finmacjones/HeltecV4.2MultiBoot
messages.content_html: A firmware selector for the Heltec WiFi LoRa 32 V4.2 (ESP32-S3) that lets you switch between multiple firmwares at boot time — Meshtastic, MeshCore, Reticulum/RNode, or anything else. https://github.com/Finmacjones/HeltecV4.2MultiBoot
embeds_html:
GitHub - Finmacjones/HeltecV4.2MultiBoot: Multi-firmware boot selec...
Multi-firmware boot selector for Heltec WiFi LoRa 32 V4.2 (ESP32-S3) - Finmacjones/HeltecV4.2MultiBoot

messages.author: Kevin
messages.text: This is awesome, does anything like this exist for a Wio Tracker L1 pro? If not I may need to get a Heltec too (edited)
messages.content_html: This is awesome, does anything like this exist for a Wio Tracker L1 pro? If not I may need to get a Heltec too (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: FinMachJones
messages.text: Not from me, but i do want to expand at some point.
messages.content_html: Not from me, but i do want to expand at some point.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: ccw
messages.text: Nice. Out of curiosity, is there anything that in principle precludes the possibility of a universal FW?
messages.content_html: Nice. Out of curiosity, is there anything that in principle precludes the possibility of a universal FW?
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: FinMachJones
messages.text: This was actually the reason i made this, I wanted to run everything under one FW to mix and match without rebooting but this is what ive done for now.
messages.content_html: This was actually the reason i made this, I wanted to run everything under one FW to mix and match without rebooting but this is what ive done for now.
messages.reply_html:
ccw
Nice. Out of curiosity, is there anything that in principle precludes the possibility of a universal FW?

messages.author: ccw
messages.text: I like it. Assuming the eventually settle in to complementary niches and both remain useful, not having to reflash is a big win
messages.content_html: I like it. Assuming the eventually settle in to complementary niches and both remain useful, not having to reflash is a big win
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: Kevin
messages.text: This will work with Heltec V4.3.1 right? https://store.rokland.com/products/heltec-wifi-lora-32v4-esp32s3-sx1262-lora-node-meshtastic-lorawan
messages.content_html: This will work with Heltec V4.3.1 right? https://store.rokland.com/products/heltec-wifi-lora-32v4-esp32s3-sx1262-lora-node-meshtastic-lorawan

messages.author: FinMachJones
messages.text: It should do, as long as the firmware you use in the slots is correct fro the board. Please let me know how it goes though, I only have tested on the v4.2
messages.content_html: It should do, as long as the firmware you use in the slots is correct fro the board. Please let me know how it goes though, I only have tested on the v4.2
